title: My ML models statistical observation on BTC for next 4 days (previous one was a good profit)
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/rqywl7wpe9mg1.png?width=809&format=png&auto=webp&s=e30ca0b86196bc319f1c43316f89a4dea94f5ce3

Hey guys,

Back with another signal from my BTC ML model — the model called a long from 24 Feb to 27 Feb and it played out really well, happy with that result. Highest was around 4,000 points return. 

( [https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/comments/1rcn1kd/my\_ml\_model\_prediction\_for\_next\_4\_days/?utm\_source=share&utm\_medium=web3x&utm\_name=web3xcss&utm\_term=1&utm\_content=share\_button](https://www.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1rcn1kd/my_ml_model_prediction_for_next_4_days/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web3x&utm_name=web3xcss&utm_term=1&utm_content=share_button) )



Here's the new one:

* **Pair :** BTC/USD
* **Direction :** SHORT / SELL
* **Entry :** 01 Mar 2026 -- 00:00 UTC
* **Exit :** 04 Mar 2026 -- 23:59 UTC         (4 days holding)
* **SL/TPNone** \- directional call only

Same as always, the model just predicts direction over the window. No SL/TP as we manage risk according to our historical data. Will post the result once it closes.

(To trade 1.0 lot one must have $50,000; 0.10 lot with $5,000)

Now, why do I even bother posting these publicly? Honestly, I'll be real with you - I don't have enough capital right now to turn these signals into life changing returns on my own. But I figured, if the model is genuinely good, the best thing I can do is post every single call in real time and build a track record out in the open. No editing, not fabricating bad calls. If it's wrong, I'll post that too.

Maybe it builds into something, maybe it doesn't. But at least the history will be there for anyone to see.

Will update once the window closes. Let's see if we can make it two in a row! 



**Not financial advice. Do your own research.**

replies.author: Paul_Kuhn
replies.body:
"Thanks in advance" 
That is an old abbreviation that was very common in the early days of the Internet. I am a bit older, my usenet time began 1981.
